exemplar.title: The exploitative and deliberate processes of governments in order to gain power.

detailedExemplarData.title: The exploitative and deliberate processes of governments in order to gain power.

strengths: Consistent focus on the prescribed title, clearly stated at the outset
Use of relevant examples from the human sciences (Bail et al. polarization study, Chinese censorship) and the arts (Vermeerâs camera obscura, personal 3-D printing experience)
Clear identification of opposing viewpoints, such as benefits of broader access to information versus risks of echo chambers
Coherent narrative tying technological change to shifts in artistic practice
weaknesses: Thesis lacks precision and rigorous definition of âknowledgeâ in each area
Analysis is uneven and frequently descriptive, with key concepts underâexplained
Supporting evidence is occasionally anecdotal and missing scholarly grounding (e.g., confirmation bias theory)
Evaluation of contrasting perspectives is brief and not fully integrated into the argument
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay demonstrates a focused engagement with the title, structured arguments and appropriate examples, indicating performance at the lower end of band 5â6. However, its uneven development, reliance on anecdote, lack of precise conceptual analysis and limited critical evaluation align more closely with band 3â4 descriptors. To respect the examinerâs final total mark of 4, the submission is awarded 4 points, reflecting solid but underdeveloped achievement in understanding knowledge questions.

annotations.comment: The student correctly identifies and states the prescribed title at the outset, ensuring clarity of focus.
The introductory paragraph presents general assertions about tools and knowledge but lacks a clear thesis that defines how âknowledgeâ will be considered in each area of knowledge.
Consider restructuring this section to move swiftly from general claims to a precise thesis statement that outlines your argument for both human sciences and the arts.
The student accurately highlights how social media tools proliferate misinformation, illustrating a valid human sciences concern.
Avoid using personal smartphone usage figures and colloquial phrases like âYikes.â Instead, cite broader studies on screen time to maintain academic tone.
The discussion of confirmation bias is pivotal but lacks a citation or deeper theoretical grounding; integrate a reference to cognitive bias research.
The case study by Bail et al. is an effective, relevant example illustrating the intensification of polarization on social media.
The analysis notes Republicans becoming more conservative but does not explain why that asymmetry occurred; elaborate on the psychological mechanisms involved.
The claim that emotive messaging is used more aggressively by one party would benefit from comparative data or a counterexample to avoid bias.
The example of Chinese government regulation aptly demonstrates how tools can be used to control knowledge, strengthening the argument.
Consider briefly addressing how government censorship differs ethically and epistemically from social-media echo chambers in democratic contexts.
The reflection on reduced depth of reading effectively highlights a societal trend but reiterates earlier points; consider consolidating.
The student insightfully recognizes the gap between tool availability and usersâ ability to learn effectively with them.
The section on art tools offers strong examples (X-rays, infrared reflectography) but the sentence on Vermeer ends abruptly; complete your thought.
Consider contrasting the technical insights gained through scientific imaging with debates on artistic interpretation to deepen analysis.
The student provides a coherent narrative of artâs evolution since Vermeer, linking technological change to shifting artistic values.
The personal example of using 3D printing and software is relevant but would carry more weight if tied back to specific conceptual definitions of artistic knowledge.
Check for minor typos and clarity in âI can write essays ease.â Proofreading will enhance professionalism.
The observation that modern tools can bypass traditional technical skill effectively highlights tensions in the arts.
The conclusion states that tool mastery is needed but remains broad; specify concrete critical-thinking strategies students should employ.
The pairing of human sciences and arts in the conclusion effectively synthesizes earlier points, reinforcing the essayâs holistic view.
The statement âexpanded our capabilitiesâ¦ but not our knowledge to doâ is intriguing but could be rephrased for clarity.
To strengthen the call to action, include practical examples: e.g., workshops on algorithm literacy or art-making techniques without automation.
The bibliography demonstrates engagement with social-science research but lacks art-focused references that would balance disciplines.
Reference style varies between a DOI and raw URL; standardize formatting per IB guidelines to ensure consistency.
Consider adding an art-history source on Vermeer or technical analysis techniques to deepen the discussion of art tools.

strands.name:

Knowledge	
Questions
strands.content: The discussion is weakly connected to the title. While there may be links to the areas of knowledge, any relevant points are descriptive or consist only of unsupported assertions.

strands.name:
Understanding	
Knowledge	
Questions
strands.content: The discussion is connected to the title and makes superficial or limited links to areas of knowledge. The discussion is largely descriptive. Limited arguments are offered but they are unclear and are not supported by effective examples.

strands.content: The discussion is focused on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and supported by examples. There is awareness and some evaluation of different points of view.

strands.name:
Understanding	
Knowledge	
Questions
strands.content: The discussion has a sustained focus on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and effectively supported by specific examples. The implications of arguments are considered. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view.
